data.content:
# Implementation plan

## Goal

Update the parent-planning workflow prompt so agents create child knots
without surprising themselves with a hierarchy_progress_blocked error
when transitioning the parent from `planning` to `plan_review`.

## Background

- Hierarchy gate (`src/state_hierarchy.rs:54-106`): when a parent
  transitions to a non-terminal target state, every direct child whose
  effective rank is below the target rank becomes a blocker. Ranks live
  in `effective_state_rank` (`src/state_hierarchy.rs:319-343`).
- A child created with the default `autopilot` profile starts in
  `planning` (rank 1). A parent moving from `planning` to `plan_review`
  (rank 3) is therefore blocked until every freshly-created child is
  itself at rank >= 3.
- A child created with `autopilot_no_planning` starts in
  `implementation` (rank 5). Such a child never blocks the parent's
  plan_review or ready_for_implementation transitions.
- The CLI already supports the safe paths:
  - `kno q "<title>"` quick-creates with the configured default quick
    profile (typically `autopilot_no_planning`; see
    `src/app/tests_workflow_roots.rs:64`).
  - `kno new "<title>" -f` (`--fast`) uses the same default quick
    profile (see `src/cli.rs:240`).
  - `kno new "<title>" -p autopilot_no_planning` is the explicit form.
- `kno edge add <parent> parent_of <child>` links after creation.
- The runtime prompt agents see is built from
  `loom/work_sdlc/prompts/planning.md` and embedded as
  `loom/work_sdlc/dist/bundle.json` via `make loom-bundle`
  (`src/loom_compat_bundle.rs:5`). Both must be updated for the change
  to reach claimed agents.

## Steps

1. Edit `loom/work_sdlc/prompts/planning.md`:
   - Replace step 7 in the `## Actions` list with explicit guidance:
     - Use `kno q "<title>"` (or `kno new "<title>" -f` /
       `kno new "<title>" -p autopilot_no_planning`) for
       implementation child knots so they start in `implementation`
       and do not block the parent's plan_review transition.
     - Recommended order: create child knots unlinked first, then add
       the parent_of edge with `kno edge add <parent> parent_of
       <child>` once the child is created.
     - Note: do NOT use the default `autopilot` profile for child
       knots created during planning. A child in `planning` (rank 1)
       blocks the parent's `plan_review` transition (rank 3) via the
       hierarchy gate.
   - Add a short "## Hierarchy Gate" sub-section under
     `## Step Boundary` (or inline in step 7) that summarises the
     rule: a child's effective state rank must be >= the parent's
     target state rank for the parent to advance.
   - Keep existing guarded-transition language and the
     "Creating child knots is planning output only" boundary intact.
2. Regenerate the runtime bundle so agents actually see the new text:
   `make loom-bundle` (writes
   `loom/work_sdlc/dist/bundle.json`). Commit the regenerated file.
3. Add a regression test in `src/loom_compat_prompt_resolution_tests.rs`
   asserting that the embedded `planning` prompt body contains
   "autopilot_no_planning" (or "--fast") and references
   `kno edge add` so the guidance cannot silently regress to the old
   short body. Use the existing
   `loom_compat_bundle::prompt_body_for_state("planning")` helper.
4. Add a changeset under `.changeset/` describing the prompt
   clarification (patch-level; documentation-only behaviour change for
   agents).
5. Verify with `make sanity` (fmt, clippy + file-size check, tests,
   coverage gate at `.ci/coverage-threshold.txt`).

## File changes (expected)

- `loom/work_sdlc/prompts/planning.md` — prompt update.
- `loom/work_sdlc/dist/bundle.json` — regenerated artifact.
- `src/loom_compat_prompt_resolution_tests.rs` — new assertion.
- `.changeset/<slug>.md` — patch changeset.

## Test strategy

- Unit/integration: new assertion in
  `loom_compat_prompt_resolution_tests.rs` covering the embedded
  bundle text.
- Existing tests in `src/prompt_tests.rs`,
  `src/loom_compat_prompt_resolution_tests.rs`,
  `src/app/tests_hierarchy*.rs`, and
  `tests/cli_state_hierarchy.rs` must continue to pass; the
  hierarchy gate semantics are not changing.
- `make sanity` (fmt, clippy, file-size, tests, coverage) is the
  pre-push gate.

## Complexity & risks

- Complexity: small. Documentation/prompt change plus a single
  regression test. No code-logic changes; hierarchy gate semantics
  remain intact.
- Risks:
  - Stale `bundle.json`: if `make loom-bundle` is skipped, agents
    keep seeing the old short body. Mitigation: explicit step 2 and
    test in step 3 that reads from the embedded bundle.
  - Over-reach: tempting to also fix the long-standing bundle/.md
    drift seen between commits 932726fa and 0be10493. Out of scope
    here; this knot only needs the planning prompt clarified. If
    `make loom-bundle` rewrites unrelated prompt bodies because of
    that drift, re-scope the change to planning-only edits and file
    a separate knot for the wider bundle refresh.
  - Profile-name drift: if `autopilot_no_planning` is renamed in
    future, the prompt and the new test will need updating. Acceptable
    — the test is a one-line string check.

## Acceptance mapping

- "Parent planning prompts explicitly say whether child knots should
  be created with autopilot-no-planning/--fast or a managed skill" →
  step 1 names all three CLI forms.
- "create unlinked child knots using autopilot-no-planning, then link
  them to the parent" → step 1 documents that exact order with
  `kno edge add <parent> parent_of <child>`.
- "hierarchy gates no longer surprise agents by blocking parent
  planning completion on newly-created child knots still waiting for
  planning" → step 1's Hierarchy Gate sub-section explains the rank
  rule and why `autopilot_no_planning` (rank 5) avoids the block.
- "existing guarded transition semantics remain intact" → no changes
  to `src/state_hierarchy.rs` or workflow.loom; step 5 verifies via
  the existing test suite.

## Hierarchy of knots

No child knots needed. This is a single, small documentation/prompt
change with one new assertion; one implementation session is
sufficient.
